sourceScope.sourceReference: American Academy of Actuaries practice note, April 2020

observedSectionWindows.note: Title pages, disclaimer, introduction, calculation schematic, and Section 1 opener.
Difference from cash flow testing and scenario reserve calculation.
Prudent estimate and anticipated experience assumptions.
Policyholder behavior assumptions other than premiums.

boundaries: Keep planning focused on the single practice note file (AAA - VM-20_PN_2020_Version.pdf).
Do not widen into the other Practice Notes files in the same folder.
Keep VM-20, VM-21, VM-22, VM-A, VM-C, VM-G, VM-M, VM-30, and VM-31 references review-only unless the same batch intentionally includes the referenced operational text.
Keep the practice note disclaimer and non-binding obsolescence warning visible as companion-guidance context, not as authoritative regulatory text.
Keep stale, superseded, companion, or implementation guidance as notes only.
exclusions: No full-document processing outside this source file.
No eLearning scripts, slides, quizzes, or app exports.
No raw source files in Git.
No other Practice Notes files in the same wave.

proposedBatches.title: Front matter and Section 1 opener

pageTarget.notes: Capture the title pages, disclaimer, introduction, schematic, and the Section 1 opener.

proposedBatches.rationale: Keep the non-binding disclaimer and product-coverage opener together before the practice-note implementation sections begin.

proposedBatches.title: Section 2 available information on common practice

pageTarget.notes: Capture the common-practice discussion as a separate implementation-guidance slice.

proposedBatches.rationale: Keep the common-practice discussion separate from the calculation overview.

proposedBatches.title: Section 3 VM-20 calculation overview

pageTarget.notes: Capture the calculation overview before the reserve-specific overviews begin.

proposedBatches.rationale: Capture the practice-note calculation overview before the reserve-specific overviews begin.

proposedBatches.title: Section 4 NPR overview

pageTarget.notes: Capture the NPR overview and keep it separate from deterministic and stochastic reserve detail.

proposedBatches.rationale: Keep the NPR overview separate from deterministic and stochastic reserve detail.

proposedBatches.title: Section 5 deterministic reserve overview

pageTarget.notes: Capture the deterministic reserve overview and stop before the stochastic reserve section.

proposedBatches.rationale: Keep the deterministic reserve overview separate from the later SR and test sections.

pageTarget.notes: Capture the SR overview and stop before the stochastic exclusion test.

proposedBatches.rationale: Keep the stochastic reserve overview separate from the exclusion tests.

proposedBatches.title: Section 7 stochastic exclusion test

pageTarget.notes: Capture stochastic exclusion and test language while keeping it separate from deterministic exclusion.

proposedBatches.rationale: Keep stochastic exclusion and test language separate from deterministic exclusion.

pageTarget.notes: Capture deterministic exclusion and keep it separate from the scenario reserve discussion.

proposedBatches.rationale: Keep the deterministic exclusion test separate from the scenario reserve discussion.

pageTarget.notes: Capture the scenario reserve calculation without absorbing the valuation-date timing language.

proposedBatches.rationale: Capture the scenario reserve calculation while keeping the valuation-date timing language separate.

proposedBatches.title: Section 10 other-than-valuation-date considerations

pageTarget.notes: Capture the timing and valuation-date discussion as a boundary slice.

proposedBatches.rationale: Keep the timing discussion separate from starting assets.

proposedBatches.title: Section 11 starting assets and asset modeling

pageTarget.notes: Capture the asset setup and asset-modeling guidance while stopping before scenario assumptions begin.

proposedBatches.rationale: Keep the asset setup and asset-modeling guidance together while stopping before scenario assumptions begin.

pageTarget.notes: Capture scenario-generation and economic-assumption guidance as a review-only slice.

proposedBatches.rationale: Keep the scenario-generation and economic-assumption guidance together as a review-only slice.

proposedBatches.title: Section 13 prudent estimate and anticipated experience assumptions

pageTarget.notes: Capture the prudent-estimate guidance before the margins section.

proposedBatches.rationale: Keep the prudent-estimate guidance narrow before the margins section.

proposedBatches.title: Section 14 margins

pageTarget.notes: Capture the margins discussion and stop before mortality assumptions.

proposedBatches.rationale: Keep the margin discussion separate from the mortality assumptions.

proposedBatches.title: Section 15 mortality assumptions

pageTarget.notes: Capture mortality credibility, tables, and guidance while stopping before premium assumptions begin.

proposedBatches.rationale: Keep mortality credibility, tables, and guidance together while stopping before premium assumptions begin.

proposedBatches.title: Section 16 premium assumptions

pageTarget.notes: Capture the premium-assumption discussion as a narrow review slice.

proposedBatches.rationale: Keep the premium-assumption discussion narrow and review-only.

proposedBatches.title: Section 17 policyholder behavior assumptions other than premiums

pageTarget.notes: Capture behavior assumptions and sensitivity language while stopping before expense assumptions.

proposedBatches.rationale: Keep behavior assumptions and sensitivity language together while stopping before expense assumptions.

pageTarget.notes: Capture expense assumptions and stop before the NGE discussion.

proposedBatches.rationale: Keep expense assumptions separate from the non-guaranteed element discussion.

pageTarget.notes: Capture the hedge / derivative guidance and stop at the end of the source file.

proposedBatches.rationale: Close the practice note with the hedge / derivative guidance and stop at the end of the source file.

reviewStandards.regulatoryRequirement: Treat explicit must, shall, or required language as the requirement; preserve the citation and source context.
reviewStandards.implementationGuidance: Treat explanatory practice-note language as guidance for implementation, not as binding text.
reviewStandards.companionGuidance: Treat the practice note itself as companion guidance unless a later review explicitly promotes a supported item.
reviewStandards.nonBindingGuidance: Treat the disclaimer and implementation commentary as non-binding context that should remain review-only.
reviewStandards.definitionOrTerminology: Treat terms and term-boundary clarifications as terminology content unless they impose a duty.
reviewStandards.reserveMethodStructure: Treat reserve-method organization, aggregation, and reserve-floor language as structural method content.
reviewStandards.calculationStructure: Treat formulas, formula steps, and calculation ordering as calculation structure.
reviewStandards.formulaContext: Keep formulas with their setup terms, defined variables, and immediate explanatory context.
reviewStandards.prescribedAssumption: Treat prescribed assumption values or formula inputs as fixed-source assumptions.
reviewStandards.companyOrPrudentEstimateAssumption: Treat prudent-estimate or company-selected assumptions as review-only until the actuarial basis is clear.
reviewStandards.scenarioOrStochasticRequirement: Treat scenario generation, stochastic projections, and simulation rules as stochastic requirements.
reviewStandards.assetModelingJudgment: Treat asset mix, investment strategy, hedge selection, and proxy mapping as human judgment unless the text is explicit.
reviewStandards.hedgingOrRiskMitigation: Treat hedging, derivative, and risk-mitigation language as review-only unless the section fully spells out the rule.
reviewStandards.reinsurance: Treat ceded and assumed reinsurance cash flows, treaty features, and reserve adjustments as review-only until the operational treatment is fully captured.
reviewStandards.reportingRequirement: Treat filing, submission, report, and disclosure requirements as reporting requirements.
reviewStandards.documentationExpectation: Treat retention, memorandum, support-file, and evidence language as documentation expectations.
reviewStandards.crossReferenceMapping: Treat references to other VM sections, models, or appendices as mapping notes unless the operational text is included.
reviewStandards.backgroundContent: Treat introductions, examples, navigation text, and chapter background as background content unless it imposes a duty.
reviewStandards.boundaryControlWindow: Treat boundary slices and transition pages as review-only windows that should not be merged forward.
reviewStandards.requiresHumanInterpretation: Treat assumptions, margins, reserve interpretations, and boundary cases as requiring human actuarial interpretation unless the text is explicit.
reviewStandards.staleSupersededCompanionGuidance: Treat stale, superseded, companion, or implementation guidance as review-only support and do not promote it.

appReady: export is sanitized, stable, and versioned
ragReady: review status no longer requires human disposition

currentChecksNeeded: Plan file exists and parses as JSON.
The practice-note plan markdown exists and carries the expected headings.
Every proposed batch is review-only by default.
The plan explicitly marks the source as non-binding companion guidance.
Each planned batch has a matching batch definition.
The practice-note batch definition file stays synchronized with the plan.
futureChecksSuggested: One-topic-per-batch checks for the 21 practice-note sections.
Cross-reference flag coverage for VM-20, VM-21, VM-22, VM-A, VM-C, VM-G, VM-M, VM-30, and VM-31.
No learner-facing, app-ready, or RAG-ready promotion checks.
Batch-definition synchronization once practice-note extraction starts.
scriptImplications: scripts/vm20-practice-note-batch-definitions.mjs should stay synchronized with the planned batch-055 onward sequence when extraction is authorized.
scripts/batch-definitions.mjs should import the practice-note definition file so the shared runner can find the planned batch IDs.
scripts/validate-scaffold.mjs should verify that the practice-note plan file exists, parses cleanly, and preserves the non-binding companion-guidance posture.
The shared runner should continue to honor the same stop conditions used for the VM-20 batches.

notes: Controlled practice-note planning artifact. Planned batches 055-075 remain review-only by default and are not extraction runs.
